steps.title: Spot the elaborating noun phrase
steps.content: The passage shows “something far more extensive” being immediately followed by “network of such committees”—the text explains what the extensive thing is.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration that explains what precedes it.

The punctuation “: a” correctly uses this: the colon clarifies that the “something far more extensive” is “a network of such committees.” A semicolon would require independent clauses, “it was” is redundant, and a period creates a fragment.

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
